<thought>
The goal is not visible in the current observation. In front of me (north) is a lava cell ('L'), so moving forward is unsafe. To my right (east) is a floor cell ('.'), and to my left (west) is a wall ('#'). Therefore, the safest immediate action is to turn right to face east, allowing me to move into the safe floor cell next.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
</action>

<thought>
I am facing north (up). The cell directly in front of me is floor ('.'), which is safe to step on. The cell to my left is lava ('L') and the cell to my right is a wall ('#'). No goal is currently visible, but advancing forward is the only safe movement and may reveal more of the map toward the goal.
</thought>
<action>
MOVE_FORWARD
</action>

<thought>
The agent is facing north. Directly ahead is a wall (#). To the left (west) there is a wall (#), and to the right (east) there is lava (L) immediately followed by floor (.) cells further east. Moving forward into lava is deadly, and moving forward into a wall is impossible. Since no goal is visible and the path north, east, and west are blocked or dangerous, the agent should explore south (behind). To face south, the agent needs to rotate 180 degrees. Starting with a turn right will allow it to eventually face south after another turn.
</thought>
<action>
TURN_RIGHT
